---
package: "openpipeline_spatial"
version: "v0.5.0"
title: "join_graphs"
namespace: "Neighbors"
description: "Combine spatial and expression neighborhood graphs into a single graph for  use in downstream clustering or trajectory analysis."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline_spatial v0.5.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `join_graphs`  
Namespace: `neighbors`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial/tree/v0.5.0/src/neighbors/join_graphs)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/neighbors/join_graphs)

:::

The fusion is a linear combination of the expression connectivities 
and spatial connectivities matrices, weighted by `alpha`.

$C_{joint} = (1 - \alpha) \cdot C_{expression} + \alpha \cdot C_{spatial}$


## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/neighbors/join_graphs/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/neighbors/join_graphs).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/neighbors/join_graphs/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name                            |Description                                                                   |Attributes                                    |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`                       |Input H5MU file with pre-computed expression and spatial neighborhood graphs. |`file`, required                              |
|`--modality`                    |Modality from the input MuData file to process.                               |`string`, default: `"rna"`                    |
|`--input_obsp_expression_graph` |Key in `adata.obsp` containing the expression connectivity matrix.            |`string`, default: `"connectivities"`         |
|`--input_obsp_spatial_graph`    |Key in `adata.obsp` containing the spatial connectivity matrix.               |`string`, default: `"spatial_connectivities"` |


### Parameters

|Name      |Description                                                                                                                                                    |Attributes               |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--alpha` |Weight of the spatial graph in the connection fusion.  A value of 0 results in the original expression graph;  a value of 1 results in the spatial graph only. |`double`, default: `0.5` |


### Outputs

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                   |Attributes                                               |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output`             |Output H5MU file path.                                                                                        |`file`, required, example: `"output.h5mu"`               |
|`--output_obsp_graph`  |Key under which to store the fused connectivity matrix in `adata.obsp`.                                       |`string`, default: `"spatial_expression_connectivities"` |
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata objects. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"`                              |

## Authors


  * Jakub Majercik [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/jakubmajercik) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/jakubmajercik) (maintainer)
